sanskrit: आरुरुक्षोर्मुने: कर्म कारणमुच्यते। योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते॥
transliteration: ārurukṣor muneḥ karma kāraṇam ucyate | yogārūḍhasya tasyaiva śamaḥ kāraṇam ucyate ||
literal_meaning: For the sage who wishes to ascend to yoga, action is said to be the cause; for the same sage who has already ascended to yoga, tranquility is said to be the cause.
contextual_meaning: Two developmental stages of inner work are distinguished: (1) the striving phase where disciplined, goal-directed effort is necessary to build capacity, and (2) the integrated phase where effort naturally subsides into effortless calm. What begins as deliberate practice matures into spontaneous stillness.
psychological_state: Transition from conscious competence to unconscious competence; shift from controlled to automatic self-regulation.
trigger_scenario: A meditator who initially needs guided apps and timers eventually sits in silence without props; a novice runner who once needed playlists and metrics later jogs peacefully without devices.
anchor_text: Work builds the path; stillness becomes the path.
